resources.string: Natural numbers are used for counting objects (1, 2, 3, ...). They have a smallest number (1) but no largest. Whole numbers extend them by including zero and negatives (..., -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, ...).

For any whole numbers a, b, and c:
 ◦ Commutative: a + b = b + a, a·b = b·a.
 ◦ Associative: (a + b) + c = a + (b + c).
 ◦ Distributive: (a + b)·c = a·c + b·c.
 ◦ Zero and Unity: a + 0 = a, a·0 = 0, a·1 = a.

Definitions:
 ◦ Divisor: divides another number with no remainder.
 ◦ Prime: has only two divisors (1 and itself).
 ◦ Composite: has more than two divisors.
 ◦ Unity (1): neither prime nor composite.

Any number >1 can be written uniquely as a product of primes.
 Divisibility rules:
 ◦ By 2 or 5: check whether the last digit is even (divisibility by 2) ; whether the last digit is 5 or 0.
 ◦ By 3 or 9: sum of digits divisible by 3 or 9.
 ◦ By 6: divisible by both 2 and 3.

Definitions:
 • Common Divisor: divides all given numbers.
 • G.C.D.: largest common divisor.
 • Relatively Prime: G.C.D. = 1.
 • L.C.M.: smallest common multiple.

Relatively prime numbers have these properties:\n 1. If a number is divisible by two relatively prime numbers, it’s divisible by their product.\n 2. If ab is divisible by c and b, c are relatively prime, then a is divisible by c.
A fraction is the result of dividing two whole numbers a and b (b ≠ 0), written as a/b.

Properties:
 ◦ a/b = c/d if ad = bc.
 ◦ Multiplying or dividing numerator and denominator by the same number doesn’t change its value.
 ◦ Addition: a/b + c/d = (ad + bc)/bd.
 ◦ Division: (a/b) ÷ (c/d) = ad/bc.
A rational number can be written as a/b, where a and b are integers and b ≠ 0. All whole numbers and fractions are rational.

Decimal forms:
 ◦ Terminating: denominator has only 2s or 5s.
 ◦ Nonterminating: denominator has other primes.
 ◦ Periodic: digits or blocks repeat infinitely.

Numbers are shown on a line: positives right, negatives left.
 |a| (modulus) means distance from zero:
 ◦ |a| = a if a ≥ 0.
 ◦ |a| = -a if a < 0.

Modulus properties:
 ◦ |a| ≥ 0 and |a| = 0 only for a=0.
 ◦ |a| = |-a|.
 ◦ |ab| = |a||b|, |a/b| = |a|/|b| (b ≠ 0).
